authoritative_sources.title: Evaluation and Management Services Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS ambulatory and physician-services guidance for office and outpatient E/M visit families, medical-decision-making and time-based documentation, and the outpatient terminology that defines the lane's core record set.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: outpatient request classification and encounter-document taxonomy

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medical Record Documentation Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS compliance guidance for provider documentation sufficiency, medical-review-ready records, and failure modes such as incomplete, illegible, or unsupported documentation. It directly constrains outpatient chart-completion work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: chart completeness validation and evidence sufficiency review

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medicare Signature Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS workflow guidance for signatures, attestations, and signature logs that determines whether outpatient notes, orders, and supporting records are administratively usable downstream.
used_for: traceability rules for addenda and signature logs

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Record Maintenance & Access Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS provider guidance for retaining, maintaining, and furnishing medical records. It is directly tied to outpatient record retrieval, audit readiness, and document-access handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record maintenance, storage, and retrieval handling

authoritative_sources.title: Individuals' Right under HIPAA to Access their Health Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's access guidance is authoritative for designated-record-set scope, timeliness, third-party transmission, and denial limits. Those rules directly constrain outpatient ROI and portal-access workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.526 - Amendment of protected health information
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Binding federal amendment rule for when a covered entity must act on a correction request, when it may deny, and how statements of disagreement or rebuttal attachments must be handled. It directly governs outpatient amendment boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Information Blocking
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy/Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC's official information-blocking guidance governs electronic health information sharing and the limited exceptions that may justify delaying or withholding access. It materially changes outpatient portal-release and disclosure workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: electronic-access exception handling and release conflict escalation
used_for: conflicting policy scenarios around portal release

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and outpatient context classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify clinic or service-line context, jurisdiction, payer, request purpose, and whether the record set is an ambulatory office or clinic packet that stays inside documentation scope rather than coding, legal, or clinical ownership.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Encounter and artifact validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate encounter notes, addenda, orders, referrals, results, scanned outside records, signatures, and amendment lineage for completeness, authentication, and source consistency across ambulatory systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and packet assembly
workflow_stages.description: Prepare completeness summaries, update notes, referral or continuity packets, ROI support packages, and audit-ready exception notes without deciding the underlying coding, clinical, or legal question.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Access, amendment, retention, and governance escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate designated-record-set access conflicts, amendment denials, information-blocking exception questions, retention questions, and conflicting record sources rather than overresolving them in an administrative lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Office and clinic encounter notes, including addenda and late entries
Orders, referrals, and associated supporting note references
Lab, imaging, and other diagnostic result documents or routed attachments
Scanned outside records and document-index metadata
After-visit summaries and patient-facing visit documentation when retained as part of the chart
Release-of-information requests, proxy or authorized-representative records, and disclosure logs
Designated-record-set access request records and portal-release exception notes
Amendment or correction requests, statements of disagreement, and rebuttal attachments
Signature attestations, signature logs, and authentication evidence
Coding-support or audit-support packets tied to the record without transferring coding authority
systems_of_record: Ambulatory EHR or clinical documentation platform
Document imaging repository or health information management system
Patient portal and release-of-information workflow system
Referral, order, or care-coordination work queue
Lab, radiology, or health-information-exchange result interfaces
Coding or charge-review work queue for supporting documentation context
Audit logging and retention control systems

allowed: Classify scope, validate completeness, and prepare outpatient documentation summaries, update notes, and evidence packets.
Identify missing, unsigned, stale, or conflicting outpatient records and route the request to the correct owner or adjacent lane.
Apply stricter tenant controls when they narrow but do not contradict the public baseline.
escalate: Service line, jurisdiction, payer, proxy authority, designated-record-set scope, or information-blocking exception posture is unknown and affects the governing record set.
Retention, access, amendment, or release rules conflict across public guidance, tenant policy, or local workflow.
Encounter notes, orders, results, or document indexes conflict across systems and source precedence is unresolved.
The request requires clinician, coder, privacy, legal, compliance, or formal approver authority.
refuse: Requests to backdate, delete, conceal, or materially rewrite official outpatient records.
Requests to approve coding level, claim submission posture, medical necessity language, or legal sufficiency outside documentation scope.
Requests that require clinical, legal, privacy-law, information-blocking, safety-critical, or policy-override authority.

domain_failure_modes: Treating outpatient documentation as generic admin work and omitting encounter-note, order, result, or referral packet dependencies that actually govern ambulatory workflows.
Certifying completeness without confirming signature status, addendum lineage, or which ambulatory system is the source of truth.
Using chart-completion authority to alter clinical authorship, decide E/M level, or influence claim or audit outcomes.
Releasing portal or ROI records without resolving designated-record-set scope, proxy authority, or documented information-blocking exception posture.
Ignoring scanned outside records, result-routing artifacts, or referral support attachments that make outpatient documentation materially different from generic document handling.
Assuming tenant policy may override the public baseline without an explicit conflict review and recorded escalation path.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific retention windows and proxy or minor-access rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Exact retention periods, proxy access rights, and who may authorize release vary materially by jurisdiction and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction-specific policy retrieval and escalate when state law or tenant policy changes the release, retention, or amendment outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant portal-release timing and sensitive-result segmentation model
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenants may use immediate release, delayed release, or segmented handling for certain result classes, which changes access packaging and exception posture.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat portal-release policy and sensitive-result segmentation as required tenant facts before asserting access readiness.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source-of-truth ownership for ambient documentation, scribe text, and scanned outside records
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different ambulatory tenants vary on when ambient or scribe-generated drafts become official records and whether scanned documents outrank interface copies.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant source-precedence mapping and owner confirmation before final completeness or amendment statements.
